content.text:
Checking how the doctor validates the socket and whether we should add a mount probe:

[REDACTED]

Identifying root cause: Colima issue #997 — with Colima’s default VZ + virtiofs, bind-mounting the macOS host path `~/.colima/.../docker.sock` makes the daemon try to create that mount source and fail with `mkdir ... docker.sock: operation not supported`. `ghr` resolves the socket via `test -S` (which passes on the host) but uses that same path in `-v`, which breaks. Colima’s maintainer recommends using `/var/run/docker.sock` (the socket inside the Linux VM) for the volume source when running `docker run` from the Mac.

[REDACTED]

input.name: macOS Colima socket mount
input.overview: The failure is not a missing Docker socket on the Mac: it is a known Colima + virtiofs limitation when bind-mounting the **host** path `~/.colima/.../docker.sock`. `ghr` should use the VM-local socket path `/var/run/docker.sock` for `-v` on macOS when the resolved API socket lives under `~/.colima/`, matching upstream Colima guidance.
input.plan:
# Fix macOS Colima Docker socket bind mount (exit 125)

## Root cause

- **`ghr doctor` passes** because [`EffectiveDockerSocket`](internal/runner/docker.go) only checks that a path exists and is a socket on the SSH session: `test -S` on `/Users/.../.colima/default/docker.sock` succeeds.
- **`ghr up` fails** at `docker run ... -v /Users/.../.colima/default/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock` with:
  `mkdir /Users/.../.colima/default/docker.sock: operation not supported` (Docker exit **125**).

This matches [Colima issue #997](https://github.com/abiosoft/colima/issues/997): with **virtiofs** (common with **VZ**), bind-mounting the **macOS host** Colima client socket path breaks; the daemon ends up doing `mkdir` on the socket path and gets `ENOTSUP`. The Colima maintainer’s fix is to mount **`/var/run/docker.sock`** (the socket **inside the VM** where `dockerd` runs), not the host path under `~/.colima/...`.

So: **CLI resolution path ≠ safe volume source path** for Colima on modern defaults.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph mac [macOS host]
    sock["~/.colima/.../docker.sock"]
    testS["test -S succeeds"]
    sock --> testS
  end
  subgraph vm [Colima Linux VM]
    vmSock["/var/run/docker.sock"]
  end
  bad["-v host .colima path ... ENOTSUP virtiofs"]
  good["-v /var/run/docker.sock ... OK"]
  sock --> bad
  vmSock --> good
```

## Code change (focused)

**File:** [`internal/runner/docker.go`](internal/runner/docker.go)

1. Add a small helper, e.g. `darwinDockerBindSourcePath(resolvedSocket string) string`, that:
   - On **darwin only** (caller ensures OS): if `resolvedSocket` contains `"/.colima/"` and looks like a Docker socket (suffix `docker.sock`), return **`DefaultDockerSocket`** (`/var/run/docker.sock`) for the **host side of `-v`**.
   - Otherwise return `resolvedSocket` unchanged.

2. In [`startDocker`](internal/runner/docker.go) for `h.OS == "darwin"`, after `EffectiveDockerSocket`, pass **`darwinDockerBindSourcePath(sockPath)`** into `darwinDockerSockFlags`, not the raw resolved path.

3. **Do not change** `EffectiveDockerSocket`’s probing order for CLI/context purposes; only the **bind mount source** used in `docker run` changes for Colima-style paths.

4. **Tests:** Extend [`internal/runner/docker_test.go`](internal/runner/docker_test.go) to assert that for a darwin host and a resolved path like `/Users/me/.colima/default/docker.sock`, the generated `-v` uses `/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock`, while non-Colima paths stay unchanged.

## Docs (brief)

Update [`docs/content/host-setup.md`](docs/content/host-setup.md) and [`docs/content/guides/linux-on-macos.md`](docs/content/guides/linux-on-macos.md) with one short note: Colima + virtiofs cannot reliably bind-mount the **host** `~/.colima/.../docker.sock`; **ghr** uses `/var/run/docker.sock` for the volume when the resolved socket is under `~/.colima/`. Optional user workaround remains `colima start --mount-type sshfs` (from upstream issue) if they hit edge cases.

## User-facing workaround (until shipped)

Set nothing extra if you ship the code fix. If they need an immediate workaround without upgrading **ghr**: use **`colima start --mount-type sshfs`**, or follow Colima’s suggestion to use `-v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock` manually (same effect as the code fix).

todos.content: Add/adjust docker_test.go cases for Colima path remapping
Document Colima virtiofs vs bind source in host-setup + linux-on-macos

input.old_string:
When `ghr` starts a docker-mode runner container on Linux or macOS, it bind-mounts the Docker socket into the container at `/var/run/docker.sock` so jobs can reach the Docker daemon. **Resolving the host socket:** if `docker_socket` is not set, ghr uses the first path that exists: `/var/run/docker.sock`; otherwise the `unix://` endpoint from the host’s current default Docker context (`docker context inspect`, which matches Colima and Linux rootless when that context is active); on **macOS** only, `~/.colima/default/docker.sock` as a last resort. Set `docker_socket` when your engine uses a different path (for example a named Colima profile that is not the default context).

On **Linux**, the **`runner` user inside the container**
input.new_string:
When `ghr` starts a docker-mode runner container on Linux or macOS, it bind-mounts the Docker socket into the container at `/var/run/docker.sock` so jobs can reach the Docker daemon. **Resolving the host socket:** if `docker_socket` is not set, ghr uses the first path that exists: `/var/run/docker.sock`; otherwise the `unix://` endpoint from the host’s current default Docker context (`docker context inspect`, which matches Colima and Linux rootless when that context is active); on **macOS** only, `~/.colima/default/docker.sock` as a last resort. Set `docker_socket` when your engine uses a different path (for example a named Colima profile that is not the default context).

**Colima on macOS (virtiofs):** If that resolved path is under `~/.colima/…`, ghr still uses it to find the API over SSH, but for `docker run -v` it bind-mounts **`/var/run/docker.sock`** (the path inside the Colima VM) instead of the macOS host file under `~/.colima/…`. Bind-mounting the host Colima client socket fails on common setups with virtiofs (`operation not supported`; see [Colima #997](https://github.com/abiosoft/colima/issues/997)). If you need a manual workaround instead, you can start Colima with `colima start --mount-type sshfs`.

On **Linux**, the **`runner` user inside the container**

input.old_string: **Docker socket permissions:** macOS runtimes (Docker Desktop, OrbStack, Colima) expose a socket that is accessible to all processes — there is no host `docker` group GID issue. ghr skips `--group-add` on macOS hosts. ghr bind-mounts the host socket into the container at `/var/run/docker.sock` so jobs can reach the Docker daemon. If `docker_socket` is unset, ghr picks `/var/run/docker.sock` when present, otherwise the `unix://` path from your default Docker context (typical for Colima), otherwise `~/.colima/default/docker.sock` on macOS. Set `docker_socket` only when the default Docker context does not match the engine you want (e.g. a named Colima profile):
input.new_string: **Docker socket permissions:** macOS runtimes (Docker Desktop, OrbStack, Colima) expose a socket that is accessible to all processes — there is no host `docker` group GID issue. ghr skips `--group-add` on macOS hosts. ghr bind-mounts the Docker socket into the container at `/var/run/docker.sock` so jobs can reach the Docker daemon. If `docker_socket` is unset, ghr picks `/var/run/docker.sock` when present, otherwise the `unix://` path from your default Docker context (typical for Colima), otherwise `~/.colima/default/docker.sock` on macOS. **Colima + virtiofs:** when the resolved path is under `~/.colima/…`, ghr uses `/var/run/docker.sock` as the bind-mount source for the runner container (VM path), not the macOS host socket file, to avoid `docker run` failing with `operation not supported` ([Colima #997](https://github.com/abiosoft/colima/issues/997)); you can also use `colima start --mount-type sshfs` if needed. Set `docker_socket` only when the default Docker context does not match the engine you want (e.g. a named Colima profile):

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented.

### Code ([`internal/runner/docker.go`](internal/runner/docker.go))

- Added **`darwinDockerBindSourcePath`**: if the resolved socket is under `/.colima/` and ends with `docker.sock`, it returns **`/var/run/docker.sock`** so `docker run -v` uses the Colima VM socket (avoids virtiofs + host `~/.colima/.../docker.sock` → exit 125).
- **`startDocker`** on darwin now calls `darwinDockerSockFlags(darwinDockerBindSourcePath(sockPath))` so only the bind source changes; **`EffectiveDockerSocket`** is unchanged.

### Tests ([`internal/runner/docker_test.go`](internal/runner/docker_test.go))

- **`Test_darwinDockerBindSourcePath`** — table of Colima vs non-Colima paths.
- **`Test_darwinDockerSockFlags_colimaSocketUsesVMBindPath`** — Colima path maps to `-v /var/run/docker.sock:...`.
- **`Test_darwinDockerSockFlags_customSocket`** — uses a non-Colima path (`~/.docker/run/docker.sock`).

### Docs

- [`docs/content/host-setup.md`](docs/content/host-setup.md) — Colima/virtiofs paragraph + link to [Colima #997](https://github.com/abiosoft/colima/issues/997) and optional `sshfs` workaround.
- [`docs/content/guides/linux-on-macos.md`](docs/content/guides/linux-on-macos.md) — same behavior described inline.

`go test ./internal/runner/ -count=1 -short` passes.

**Operational note:** If the registration token in your earlier log was real, revoke/regenerate it in GitHub; it may be compromised from the paste.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Here are the logs:
➜  ~ colima start --mount-type sshfs                                                                                                                                                                                               
WARN[0002] 'volume mount type' cannot be updated after initial setup, discarded 
INFO[0002] starting colima
INFO[0002] runtime: docker
INFO[0005] starting ...                                  context=vm
INFO[0015] provisioning ...                              context=docker
INFO[0018] starting ...                                  context=docker
INFO[0020] done

It seems not working?
</user_query>

Colima is behaving as designed. That line is the important one:

**`'volume mount type' cannot be updated after initial setup, discarded`**

So **`--mount-type sshfs` is being ignored.** The VM was already created the first time you ran Colima (almost certainly with the default mount type, e.g. virtiofs for VZ). Colima does **not** let you change volume mount type on an existing VM; it only applies on **initial** creation.

So it “doesn’t work” in the sense that **you didn’t actually switch to sshfs** — Colima started the same VM as before.

### If you still want sshfs

You need a **fresh** Colima instance so mount type is chosen at setup, for example:

1. Stop and remove the current VM (this wipes that Colima profile’s disk — back up anything you care about):
   - `colima stop`
   - `colima delete` (or `colima delete -f` / profile-specific delete, depending on your Colima version and profile name)

2. Start again **with** mount type from the first start:
   - `colima start --mount-type sshfs`

(Exact flags can vary slightly by Colima version; `colima start --help` is the source of truth.)

### If your goal was only fixing `ghr` / `docker run -v …docker.sock`

The **ghr** change maps Colima’s `~/.colima/.../docker.sock` resolution to **`-v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock`**, which avoids the virtiofs host-socket bind problem **without** recreating Colima. So you may not need sshfs at all if you’re on a **ghr** build that includes that fix.

**Summary:** The warning means “not applied.” Either recreate Colima to use sshfs, or use a **ghr** version with the Colima bind-mount fix and skip the sshfs workaround.
